specifications.banking: 0-4 degrees in turns, 0 degrees on straightaways

history.historicalNotes: Originally 15km over public roads in Ardennes region. Hairpin eliminated at Eau Rouge creek. Reconfigured for safety concerns in 1980s. Continues to evolve.

notable_features: Eau Rouge corner - high-speed left-right combination
characteristics.elevationChanges: Significant elevation changes through Ardennes Mountains
characteristics.description: Longest lap of all current F1 circuits. High speeds, technical corners, weather-dependent conditions.

cornerByCornerNotes.eau_rouge: Iconic high-speed left-right combination following the long Mulsanne straight. Requires commitment and precise line.
cornerByCornerNotes.blanchimont: Fast sweeping right-hander in the high-speed section
cornerByCornerNotes.la_source: Tight opening hairpin after the opening straight

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - high-speed layout, elevation changes, weather sensitivity

history.historicalNotes: Originally RAF airfield, ad-hoc 2-mile race in 1947. Official opening 1948. Hosted inaugural F1 World Championship in 1950 (won by Giuseppe Farina). Major reconfiguration 2010-2011 with Arena section.

characteristics.description: Historic F1 circuit with high-speed character. Recently modified Arena section provides modern overtaking opportunities while maintaining legendary sections like Copse and Stowe.

cornerByCornerNotes.copse: High-speed right-hander at circuit entrance, iconic corner
cornerByCornerNotes.maggotts_becketts_chapel: Technical complex combining multiple direction changes at speed
cornerByCornerNotes.stowe: Fast left-hand sweeper, top speed corner
cornerByCornerNotes.village_loop_aintree: Modern Arena section added 2010-2011 for overtaking

currentState.recentRenovations: Arena section added 2010-2011, various safety improvements

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - historical high-speed layout with modern overtaking areas

history.historicalNotes: Opened in 1956 on public roads. Originally 6.6 miles on highway. Boot section added in 1971 for safety. Reconfigured multiple times for safety improvements.

notable_features: The Esses (Turns 2-4) - series of sweeping turns
The Boot (Turns 9-10) - hammerhead up-and-downhill section added 1971
The Inner Loop - bus-stop chicane on both layouts
characteristics.description: Historic American road course with excellent elevation changes and technical sections. Notable for The Boot's unique uphill-downhill design.

cornerByCornerNotes.the_90: Turn 1 right-hander, good passing opportunity on entry to back straight
cornerByCornerNotes.the_esses: Turns 2-4 fast sweeping apexes flowing onto back straight
cornerByCornerNotes.the_boot: Hammerhead turns 9-10 with unique elevation change, added for safety
cornerByCornerNotes.inner_loop: Bus-stop chicane configuration on both long and short layouts

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - good elevation changes, multiple layouts, technical boot section

history.historicalNotes: Opened in 1957 on former Fort Ord military base. Known for producing excellent road racing. Home to MotoGP for decades.

notable_features: Corkscrew (Turns 8-8A) - famous 130m downward slope with 33m elevation change
characteristics.elevationChanges: 180 feet (55 meters) total elevation change between highest and lowest points
characteristics.description: Famous for the Corkscrew, one of the most challenging corners in racing. Elevation changes make it unique and difficult.

cornerByCornerNotes.corkscrew: Turns 8-8A: 130-meter downward slope with 59-foot drop between entry and exit. 12% drop at Turn 8 apex, 18% at Turn 8A apex. One of racing's most feared corners.
cornerByCornerNotes.the_esses: Technical section combining multiple direction changes
cornerByCornerNotes.rahal_straight: High-speed section allowing for good speeds

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - Corkscrew is notoriously difficult, elevation changes significant

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1955 in Kettle Moraine region. Built by Cliff Tufte as 14-turn, 4-mile circuit. Maintained original configuration - one of few circuits doing so.

notable_features: The Kink - famous backside corner with optional chicane
characteristics.description: Classic American road course with high speeds, especially on front straight (approaching 200mph). Long lap provides good racing.

cornerByCornerNotes.the_kink: Famous backside turn with optional chicane configuration on second layout
cornerByCornerNotes.front_straight: Long frontstretch allowing speeds approaching 200 mph
cornerByCornerNotes.turn_1: Long right-hand corner leading onto front straight

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - good speeds but manageable for most skill levels

specifications.banking: 31 degrees in turns, 18 degrees on straightaways

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1959 as America's first superspeedway. Hosted Daytona 500 since 1959. Repaved in 2011, changing racing characteristics significantly.

characteristics.description: Legendary superspeedway known for high speeds and pack racing. Steep banking (31 degrees) allows speeds approaching 200 mph.

cornerByCornerNotes.turn_1: Highly banked right-hand turn, beginning of tri-oval
cornerByCornerNotes.turn_3: Highly banked left-hand turn, critical passing zone

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - pack racing, high speeds, tire management critical

specifications.banking: 9 degrees 12 minutes in turns, flat straights

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1909. Hosted first Indy 500 in 1911. Most prestigious American racing event. Rectangular superspeedway design.

characteristics.description: America's most famous racing venue. Rectangular shape with relatively flat banking makes it unique. Home of the Indy 500.

cornerByCornerNotes.turn_1: Turn 1, 9-degree banking, start of first short chute
cornerByCornerNotes.turn_2: Turn 2, 9-degree banking, end of front straight
cornerByCornerNotes.short_chute_1: 0.125 mile short chute between turns 1 and 2
cornerByCornerNotes.short_chute_2: 0.125 mile short chute between turns 3 and 4

currentState.recentRenovations: Ongoing facility improvements, new road course added

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - distinctive rectangular shape, good racing surface
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: Includes oval and road course configurations

history.historicalNotes: Built 1927. Also known as Green Hell. One of world's most demanding road courses. Natural undulating terrain through Eifel Mountains.

characteristics.elevationChanges: Nearly 1,000 feet elevation gain/loss per lap
characteristics.description: World's most demanding road course. Twists and turns through Eifel Mountains for nearly 13 miles with extreme elevation changes. Natural terrain creates blind corners and constant direction changes.

cornerByCornerNotes.hatzenbach: Opening section establishing the circuit's demanding character
cornerByCornerNotes.karussell: Famous banked left-hand corner, must be respected

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Expert - longest circuit in iRacing, extreme elevation, 154 turns, very demanding
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: Separated from Grand-Prix-Strecke. Extreme test of skill and car control.

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1962, originally Honda test track. Designed by John Hugenholtz. Unique figure-8 layout causes track to cross over itself.

characteristics.description: Unique figure-8 layout with track crossing over itself via overpass. Honda-owned facility. Technical mix of high-speed and slow corners.

cornerByCornerNotes.130r: Fast sweeping right-hander, demanding corner in final section
cornerByCornerNotes.first_esses: Testing series of esses at circuit start

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - figure-8 layout unique, multi-apex corners demanding

history.historicalNotes: First race 1938. Uses public roadways when not racing. Added as iRacing newest facility November 2013. 570ft/174m vertical difference between high and low points.

notable_features: Mt Panorama climb - steep hill section
characteristics.elevationChanges: 570 feet (174 meters) total elevation change, very steep
characteristics.description: Most treacherous circuit due to excessive steepness. Combines public road characteristics with racing. Famous for dramatic elevation changes and high-speed sections downhill.

cornerByCornerNotes.skyline: Fast right-hander at elevation, iconic section

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Expert - extreme elevation changes, public road characteristics, technical and high-speed sections

history.historicalNotes: Designed by Hermann Tilke. Designed December 2010, FIA approval September 2012, first F1 race 60 days later. Built on ~900 acres near Austin.

notable_features: Turn 1 - signature tight hairpin at 133ft elevation crest
characteristics.description: Modern circuit designed for multiple racing series. Signature Turn 1 at elevation provides unique challenge. Mix of technical and high-speed sections.

cornerByCornerNotes.turn_1: Tight hairpin at 133-foot elevation crest, signature corner requiring precision

currentState.recentRenovations: Scheduled for complete art overhaul in 2025

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - modern design, good racing, turn 1 hairpin challenging

history.historicalNotes: 1920s original was 10.7 miles. 1932 bypass eliminated triangular loop into town, reducing to 8.3 miles. Pit straightaway widened after 1955 disaster (83 deaths). Porsche Curves and Ford Chicanes added 1970s.

notable_features: Ford Chicanes - slow chicanes approach pits
characteristics.description: World's most famous endurance race venue. Long high-speed straights combined with technical chicanes. Demands car balance for both speed and fuel economy.

cornerByCornerNotes.mulsanne_straight: Long high-speed straight, famous for top speeds in endurance racing
cornerByCornerNotes.ford_chicanes: Mandatory slow chicanes before pits for safety

includedSeries: 24 Hours of Le Mans special event
iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - long endurance circuit, fuel/tire management critical, demanding race format
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: Special 24-hour endurance race events held annually

history.historicalNotes: 1920s motorcycle events on 3/4 mile dirt track. Post-WWII paved and modified with Druids hairpin. 1959 added mile of track for 2.3 mile full circuit. Indy circuit uses original 1.2 mile track.

characteristics.description: Historic circuit 30 miles southeast of London. Dramatic elevation changes and challenging mixture of fast, slow and medium-speed corners. Millimeter-accurate recreation in iRacing.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - elevation changes significant, Druids hairpin technical, good racing
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: Includes Indy (1.2 mi) and Grand Prix (2.4 mi) layouts. Rallycross configuration available.

specifications.banking: 24-28 degrees variable in turns, 5-9 degrees on frontstretch, 4-8 degrees on backstretch

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1961. High-banked short track known for intense fender-banging racing. Notable for variable banking design allowing steep banking with minimal speed loss between corners.

characteristics.description: Tightest oval in NASCAR Cup Series. Concrete surface provides excellent traction. Variable banking design unique. Known for side-by-side racing and frequent contact.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - tight oval, aggressive racing, concrete surface, side-by-side challenges

specifications.banking: 24 degrees in turns, 5 degrees on straightaways

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1960. Quad-oval configuration. Modern facility with various configurations for different racing types.

characteristics.description: Quad-oval intermediate speedway hosting major NASCAR events. 24-degree banking provides good racing with multiple grooves.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - quad-oval configuration, good racing surface, 24-degree banking
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: Includes oval and Roval (road/oval hybrid) configurations

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1996. Major renovation 2006 increased banking from 12 to 20 degrees. Progressive banking design modern.

characteristics.description: Modern D-shaped oval with progressive banking promoting side-by-side racing while maintaining intermediate speedway characteristics.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - graduated banking, good side-by-side racing, modern design

specifications.banking: 18 degrees in turns, 12 degrees on frontstretch, 5 degrees on backstretch

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1969. D-shaped superspeedway with 73-foot wide racing surface. High banks and wide surface enable good racing.

characteristics.description: Two-mile D-shaped superspeedway with 18-degree banking and wide racing surface. Allows multiple racing grooves and competitive racing.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - 18-degree banking, two-mile length, multiple grooves available

specifications.banking: 12 degrees in turns, 0 degrees on straights

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1947. Distinctive paperclip oval shape. One of shortest tracks in NASCAR. Historic venue with unique characteristics.

characteristics.description: Shortest track in NASCAR with distinctive paperclip shape. Tight oval with 12-degree banking. Produces competitive wheel-to-wheel racing.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Beginner - shortest NASCAR track, paperclip shape, predictable racing

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1969. Fastest closed-circuit motorsports facility in world. 33-degree banking highest in NASCAR.

characteristics.description: World's fastest closed-circuit facility. Extreme 33-degree banking produces highest speeds in NASCAR. Pack racing strategy critical.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - fastest speeds, pack racing mandatory, high banking, strategy dependent

specifications.banking: Turn 1: 14 degrees, Turn 2: 8 degrees, Turn 3: 6 degrees

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1971. Known as Tricky Triangle. Designed by Rodger Ward. Each turn borrowed from different track: Turn 1 from Trenton, Turn 2 from Indianapolis, Turn 3 from Milwaukee.

notable_features: Longest straightaway in North America (3,740 feet)
characteristics.description: Unique tri-oval with three completely different turns, each adapted from famous speedways. Longest straightaway in North America. Diverse banking angles create unique racing challenge.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - three unique turns, each requiring different approach, variable banking makes setup critical

specifications.banking: 24 degrees in turns, 8 degrees on straightaways

history.historicalNotes: Built 1954 by Earl Baltes on former Ohio cornfield. Home of World of Outlaws. Dirt oval demanding racecraft.

characteristics.description: Premier dirt oval for Sprint Cars and Midgets. Half-mile clay surface with 24-degree banking provides excellent racing conditions.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - dirt racing dynamics, 24-degree banking, tire management

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1926. Known as Sprint Car Capital of the World. Annual Chili Bowl prestigious sprint car event.

characteristics.description: Premier dirt oval for Sprint Cars. Half-mile with 7-degree banking. Home of prestigious Knoxville Nationals and Chili Bowl.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - dirt racing, 7-degree banking, tire management critical

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1976. Two-mile street circuit through coastal city. Encircles Long Beach Convention Center and Aquarium.

characteristics.description: Unique street circuit winding through Long Beach streets. 11 turns create continuous tight-loose-tight pattern. Demands precision and tire management.

cornerByCornerNotes.various_corners: 11 turns in rapid succession, demanding precision

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - street circuit, tight corners, wall-to-wall racing, no margin for error

name: Autódromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari (Imola)

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1950. Retained original counterclockwise direction until 1972 when chicanes added for safety. One of few international circuits run counterclockwise.

characteristics.description: Challenging road course with technical chicanes and demanding corners. Unique counterclockwise direction. Mix of high-speed and slow sections.

cornerByCornerNotes.various_sections: Mix of demanding corners and straights

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - technical chicanes, high-speed sections, demanding layout

specifications.banking: 0 degrees (increased banking at Turns 3 and 14 in 2020)

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1948. Designed by John Hugenholtz. Located along sand dunes near North Sea. 2020 banking added to turns 3 and 14 for F1 return.

notable_features: Sand dunes location near North Sea
characteristics.description: Unique sand dunes location. Straightaways, tight and medium-speed turns. 2020 banking addition modernized for F1 return while preserving character.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - sand dunes location unique, banking updates modern, good racing

history.historicalNotes: Built 1986 in rapid eight months. Hosted first-ever F1 event in Eastern Europe. Located just outside Budapest.

notable_features: Complex selection of slow to medium-speed turns
characteristics.description: Technical circuit with only one significant straight. Complex corner combinations require precision. Tight layout limits high-speed sections.

cornerByCornerNotes.various_corners: Complex selection of slow and medium-speed turns

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - technical layout, limited passing, demanding precision

history.historicalNotes: Historic venue hosting multiple motorsport disciplines over decades. Well-established racing facility.

characteristics.description: Historic circuit with diverse corner mix. Good elevation changes and technical sections. Multiple circuit configurations available.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - elevation changes, technical sections, good racing

history.historicalNotes: Modern facility in Japan. Known for good racing surface and technical layout.

characteristics.description: Modern Japanese circuit with technical challenging layout. Supports dynamic weather and rain racing in iRacing.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - technical layout, good racing, modern facility
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: Supports dynamic weather and rain racing. Updated 2024.

history.historicalNotes: Opened April 20, 1957. Home of Skip Barber Racing School. One of oldest continuously-operated road courses in North America.

characteristics.description: Tight road course perfect for developing skills. Home to Skip Barber Racing School. Historic venue with simple but demanding layout.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Beginner - tight circuit, good for skill development, small track

history.historicalNotes: Historic venue hosting multiple racing series. Various configurations reflect different racing needs.

characteristics.description: Versatile venue with multiple configurations. 12-turn full circuit mixed with cup and rally configurations. Supports dynamic weather and rain racing.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - multiple configurations, technical sections, good racing
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: Supports dynamic weather and rain racing. Includes rallycross configuration.

history.historicalNotes: Built 2003. Designed by Alan Wilson. Located in heavily wooded area near Birmingham. Recently underwent complete repave and infrastructure improvements.

characteristics.description: Modern track with 16-turn technical layout. 45-foot racing width provides good passing opportunities. Heavily wooded setting makes it unique.

currentState.recentRenovations: Complete repave and infrastructure rebuild, new iRacing version 2026 Season 2

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - 16-turn technical layout, elevation changes, demanding precision
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: Rebuilt from ground up 2026 Season 2 with complete repave data

history.historicalNotes: Historic venue hosting various racing disciplines. Known for technical challenging layout.

characteristics.description: Technical circuit with multiple configurations. Good elevation changes and varied corner types. Supports AI racing at all configurations.

currentState.recentRenovations: 2022 laser scan and new artwork, AI added to all configs

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - technical layout, multiple configs, elevation changes, good racing
iRacingNotes.specialNotes: AI racing available at all configurations

history.historicalNotes: Historic British circuit known for elevation changes and blind turns.

characteristics.description: Famous for numerous elevation changes and blind turns taken at high speed requiring commitment. Multiple circuit configurations available.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Advanced - elevation changes, blind turns, multiple configs, demanding layout

history.historicalNotes: Opened 1951 on former WWII RAF airfield. Built on old runways and taxi ways. Multiple circuit configurations available.

characteristics.description: Historic circuit built on RAF airfield. Multiple configurations available. 300 circuit longest at 2.98 miles. Good racing surface.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - multiple configurations, good racing, moderate technical demand

history.historicalNotes: Historic Scottish circuit. Multiple configurations available for different racing types.

characteristics.description: Scottish circuit with elevation changes. 9-turn international layout most common. Tri-oval short configuration available. Taylor's hairpin final turn.

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - elevation changes, multiple configs, good technical layout

history.historicalNotes: Current form from 2008 reconstruction. Combines three long straights with flowing mix of fast sweeping and 90-degree turns. Modern facility.

characteristics.description: Modern facility combining three long straights with technical flowing sections. 10-turn circuit with mix of fast and moderate-speed corners.

cornerByCornerNotes.various_corners: Mix of fast sweeping and 90-degree turns

iRacingNotes.difficulty: Intermediate - modern design, good racing, flowing layout
